trends.summary: Stride Ventures has obtained backing from the Public Investment Fund (PIF), highlighting growing interest in venture debt among Indian startups. Meanwhile, Indian startup funding declined to $90 million in early March, reflecting a slowdown in venture capital deal activity. This shift indicates evolving financing trends within the Indian startup ecosystem as of March 2024.

trends.trend_title: ClearToken Deploys Daml Platforms on Canton Network
trends.summary: ClearToken, an FCA-authorised digital financial market infrastructure provider, has partnered with Canton Network to deploy three Daml-based Digital Asset Platforms—CT Register, CT Pay, and CT Settle—directly on the Canton Network. This strategic collaboration aims to enhance digital asset management and settlement capabilities.

trends.summary: Nvidia-backed Nscale Global secured a $14.6 billion valuation in a fresh funding round as it prepares for an initial public offering. The company has engaged Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan as IPO underwriters, with former Meta executives Nick Clegg and Sheryl Sandberg involved in the venture.

trends.trend_title: Raymond Realty Rs 3,000 Cr Kandivali JV
trends.summary: Raymond Realty has signed a Rs 3,000 crore joint venture to develop a housing project in Kandivali, Mumbai, marking its seventh joint development deal. The company has strategically acquired 100 acres for this urban development initiative.
evidence_snippets: [ET Industry] In an exciting new chapter for urban development, Raymond Realty has teamed up to launch a promising housing project in Kandivali, Mumbai, with anticipated revenues soaring to Rs 3,000 crore.

trends.summary: Capgemini announced its acquisition of Piterion, a leading independent specialist in product lifecycle management and industrial digitalization. This strategic move aims to enhance Capgemini's capabilities in digital transformation for industrial sectors. The deal was reported in early 2024, marking a significant expansion of Capgemini's service portfolio.

trends.trend_title: Cult.fit Expands with Neo Gyms and Pilates
trends.summary: Cult.fit is broadening its fitness offerings by investing in affordable mass-market centers like Cult Neo and premium Pilates studios, alongside a rapidly growing consumer products division. This strategic expansion aims to position Cult.fit as a comprehensive active lifestyle brand competing with boutique studios and D2C fitness brands.
evidence_snippets: [Mint Companies] The company is investing in mass-market centres like Cult Neo, premium Pilates studios, and a rapidly growing consumer products division as it aims to become an active lifestyle brand.

trends.summary: Genius Group reported an 80% pro forma revenue growth, reaching $13.6 million in its 2025 financial results. The company has completed its transformation into a triple-engine AI education group with divisions Genius School, Genius Academy, and Genius Resorts, focusing on preparing the world for the future of work.
